item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My main issues are:

1) I don't understand a lick of this

2) It requires inventing too much paratech to be able to both detect and quantify what's going on here.

The ending is intriguing, but I kept looking for the hidden collapsible, hoping for further explanation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There are parts of this I really like and parts I really don't. By volume most of this is vaguely threatening and mysterious nonsense that mostly seems to be designed to fill space rather than be meaningful.

Which is a shame because I really like the base idea and its implications - Temporal Anomalies fuckery, and the kill-on-sight notice. It's just that most of the flavour put in to support it feels blandly random and actively detracts from the parts I like.

Bold to include graphs and put no colour scales or legends though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good take. Reminds me a lot of the Xeelee Sequence. The dark matter photino creatures couldn't be contained by the Xeelee and they're basically all nigh-omnipotent spacetime benders. The eternal recurrence is strongly implied.

item.title: Nice
item.content:encoded:
I'm a fan of infiltration-sudden-shutdown endings to files. I'm a fan of cosmic/physics anoms. The use of greek letters instead of the usual integers and letters makes it feel sciency. I guess I'll need to re-read it to get a better appreciation for how the infiltration happened.

Maybe, having a very short-term deletion of the file pending is a perpetual canard since, clearly, it doesn't get deleted. It's got the upvotes to stand on (deservedly). Maybe a request to delete the file is denied for some reason, combined with the liquidation of the TA dpt.

Also, I think we need an updated canon fodder file for all the dpts, there seem to be more in the 6th series than there were previously.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
You had me at "Why is there fire". :)

I feel this is an extreme reaction by the O-Team, I feel the Temporal Anomalies Department may have saved our bacon on this one. Unequivocal +1 from me

item.title: can't relate
item.content:encoded:
I can't follow what happened at all, maybe tone it down a little using psionics to link timeline events to words and emotions, I mean it looks like a strange anomalous ritual that a GOI might do.
It's just too complicated.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Haha, what the fuck. The best possible type of what the fuck, but still, what the FUCK.

After going over this one several times, I have some kind of theory, maybe?

+ show block
– hide block

So, Procedure 000/UNIVERSALIS was an attempt by a future, or the current, Foundation to send something (the worms mentioned, presumably) back in time to the dawn of the universe. They did this in order to retroactively neutralize threats they couldn’t contain- “these embryos dissolve in whimpers where they can never reach us anymore”.

However, the Temporal Anomalies Department caught wind of this. They couldn’t allow the rest of the Foundation to fuck with the timeline to such an extent for a dubiously moral end; thus, they sent a missile back in time, bombing 000/UNIVERSALIS to hell, destroying the entire Procedure and possibly causing the Big Bang in the process.

This is almost definitely high treason, so now the entire Department is being hunted down with a kill-on-sight order. But it’s worth it- the universe isn’t contained, and the Foundation can never take that away from us.

Probably completely wrong, but this was my impression. Absolutely loved this article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is wild and wonderful in several ways, for several reasons. Easily one of the best scips I've read in a while — it's genuinely amazing what you were able to produce in a couple of days.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I may be wrong, but it appears to me at some point the Temporal Dept. purposefully set off the Big Bang with the goal of killing off the Foundation. The Foundation wants to prevent this from happening again.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't exactly get what's going on here? Does someone have an idea of what's going on in the article?

Like why are the foundation hunting the Temporal Department Personnel now? Aren't they a part of their organization?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Coldposting? In my Jamcon?

It's more likely than you think.

Image source
